reasonCode.text: Pre-operative assessment for total knee replacement. Right knee OA grade IV. Pain 8/10. Approved for surgery.
Patient reports dizziness when standing. Adjusted antihypertensive dosing. Knee pain well-controlled at 2/10. Completing physical therapy.
Annual visit. Patient recently lost wife Carole (January 2025). Reports low mood, poor appetite, disrupted sleep. Initiated antidepressant therapy.

Recurring dizziness on standing, two near-falls in past month. Possible orthostatic hypotension from HCTZ + metoprolol combination. Mild dehydration suspected; serum sodium borderline elevated. Son David concerned about father living alone.

note.text: Stage 2 hypertension, managed with HCTZ, lisinopril, and metoprolol.
Managed with atorvastatin 40 mg. LDL within target range.
Managed with metformin. HbA1c borderline elevated. Dehydration worsens glucose control.
Progressed to grade IV. Total knee arthroplasty performed March 2024. Post-operative neuropathic pain managed with gabapentin.
Onset following death of spouse Carole Larson on 2025-01-08. Reports low mood, anhedonia, poor appetite, disrupted sleep, social withdrawal. Initiated sertraline 50 mg.
Uncomplicated total knee arthroplasty. Cemented prosthesis. 3-day inpatient stay. Discharged home with PT referral and gabapentin for neuropathic pain management.
20 sessions of outpatient PT. Achieved functional range of motion. Gait normalized. Patient cleared for independent ambulation.

coding.display: Metoprolol Succinate 50 MG Extended Release Oral Tablet

medicationCodeableConcept.text: Metoprolol Succinate 50 MG Extended Release Oral Tablet

dosageInstruction.text: Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily. May cause dizziness — rise slowly from sitting or lying position.

note.text: Side effects: dizziness, bradycardia, fatigue. Monitor heart rate. Advise patient not to stop abruptly.

dosageInstruction.text: Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily. May cause dizziness, especially with first dose.

note.text: ACE inhibitor. Side effects: dizziness, dry cough, hyperkalemia. Combined with HCTZ increases dehydration and dizziness risk.

dosageInstruction.text: Take 1 tablet by mouth every morning. Causes increased urination — ensure adequate fluid intake to prevent dehydration.

note.text: Thiazide diuretic. PRIMARY dehydration risk. Causes electrolyte loss, orthostatic hypotension, dizziness. Patient must drink 8+ glasses of water daily. Monitor serum sodium and potassium.

dosageInstruction.text: Take 1 capsule by mouth three times daily for neuropathic pain. Take with or without food. Avoid driving if dizzy.

note.text: Post-operative neuropathic pain control. Strong dizziness and sedation side effects especially in elderly. HIGH fall risk when combined with other CNS-active drugs. Review for dose reduction.

dosageInstruction.text: Take 1 tablet by mouth twice daily with meals.

note.text: Caution: dehydration from HCTZ can increase lactic acidosis risk with metformin. Ensure adequate hydration.

dosageInstruction.text: Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily at bedtime.
Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily in the morning with food. May cause dizziness, especially in the first few weeks.

note.text: SSRI initiated for bereavement-related depression after loss of spouse Carole. Common side effects: dizziness, nausea, insomnia. Additive dizziness risk with other medications.
Rate controlled on metoprolol.

coding.display: Pain severity - 0-10 verbal numeric rating [Score] - Reported

note.text: Right knee pain, worse with stairs and prolonged walking.

coding.display: Pain severity - 0-10 verbal numeric rating [Score] - Reported

note.text: Post-TKA, well controlled on gabapentin. Mild residual stiffness in the morning.

coding.display: Pain severity - 0-10 verbal numeric rating [Score] - Reported

note.text: Knee pain stable. Main complaint today is dizziness on standing.
Borderline hypernatremia. Consistent with mild dehydration. Patient reports not drinking enough water. HCTZ contributing to fluid loss.
Mildly elevated creatinine, consistent with pre-renal azotemia from dehydration. Trend from 1.0 (2024).
Elevated fasting glucose. Dehydration concentrating blood glucose. Reinforce hydration and metformin adherence.

reasonCode.text: Elderly patient with multimorbidity, post-knee replacement, dizziness from polypharmacy, dehydration risk, and bereavement-related depression. Living alone.

text.div: Care Plan for Mr. Frank Larson: management of hypertension, T2DM, post-TKA recovery, polypharmacy dizziness, dehydration risk, and depression.

code.text: Monitor blood pressure and orthostatic vitals at every visit
Hydration education: minimum 8 glasses water daily to counter HCTZ-induced fluid loss
Fall prevention program: dizziness review, home safety assessment, grab bars recommended
Medication reconciliation: review gabapentin dose reduction as post-op pain resolves
Mental health support: monthly check-ins for depression following bereavement
Family engagement: coordinate care updates with son David Larson (617-555-0148)
Knee rehabilitation: continue home exercise program post-PT discharge
Diabetes management: monitor blood glucose, encourage dietary adherence, annual HbA1c

note.text: Acute gout flare in right first metatarsophalangeal joint, likely precipitated by hydrochlorothiazide. Treated with colchicine and short-course indomethacin. HCTZ dose reviewed. Fully resolved after 9 weeks.

Right lower lobe pneumonia confirmed on chest X-ray. Complicated by poorly controlled type 2 diabetes. Hospitalized for 4 days; treated with IV azithromycin transitioning to oral amoxicillin-clavulanate. Pneumococcal vaccine updated. Fully resolved.
Acute viral pharyngitis. Negative rapid strep test. Managed conservatively with rest, fluids, and OTC analgesics. Resolved within 18 days.

statusReason.text: Discontinued when lisinopril was initiated for dual antihypertensive therapy with superior renal-protective effect.

dosageInstruction.text: Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily.

note.text: Added as second antihypertensive when BP remained at Stage 2 on HCTZ alone. Replaced by lisinopril on 2022-08-15 for better BP control and renoprotection given T2DM.

statusReason.text: Discontinued due to risk of NSAID-related renal impairment in the context of hypertension and type 2 diabetes.

dosageInstruction.text: Take 1 tablet by mouth twice daily with food as needed for knee pain. Do not exceed 14 days without physician review.

note.text: Prescribed for osteoarthritis knee pain. Stopped after 2 years due to concerns about nephrotoxicity and cardiovascular risk in a patient with HTN, T2DM, and CKD risk. Pain management transitioned to acetaminophen and later gabapentin.

statusReason.text: Discontinued after glycemic targets achieved on metformin monotherapy; risk of hypoglycemia outweighed benefit.

dosageInstruction.text: Take 1 tablet by mouth 30 minutes before breakfast.

note.text: Started alongside metformin at T2DM diagnosis for initial glycemic control. Discontinued in November 2014 after HbA1c stabilized; metformin alone was sufficient. Hypoglycemic episodes noted on combined therapy.
